programmes.show_description: OJ is live through the night from the Salford Riviera with Midnight Mastermind, Wish You Were There, Midnight Caller and a huge Ravio 2 to end the week.
Angela Griffin celebrates international female artists with an All Queens Unwinds Mix featuring Annie Lennox, Tracy Chapman, Cyndi Lauper and many more.
It's Early Breakfast with Owain Wyn Evans, featuring Step Back in Time and Airdrum Anthems. It's time for your 3ws! WHO, WHERE AND WHAT are YOU looking forward to this weekend?
Scott meets this year's Eurovision act, LOOK MUM, NO COMPUTER. Martin Clunes and James Buckley are on the show... and the Wonder Years start in 1978!
Britain's bestselling author and the brain behind The Gruffalo Julia Donaldson selects her final two Tracks Of My Years of the week. And two listeners take on Ten To The Top.
Jeremy and guests discuss the news headlines and talk to the people making them.
Trevor is bringing you some of the best music from across the decades to get you through the afternoon.
It's All Request Friday, and Sara wants to hear from you on 88291 or sara.cox@bbc.co.uk.
Come on, grab somebody, let the music lift you up at Michelle's Fabulous Friday party!
Michelle's Mood is...LET'S GO OUTSIDE! Your weekly 30 minutes of non-stop bops is here!
This week Sophie celebrates pop music's great drummers in the Disco Wonderland. Plus, there are anthems from Raye, Prince, Usher, Kelis, Moloko and Baccara.

programmes.show_name: The Rock Show with Shaun Keaveny
programmes.show_description: Anna Calvi chooses her Rock Goddess to mark International Women's Day... and Shaun's got an all-female tracklist.

programmes.show_description: Celebrating hip hop's women with some exclusive Maida Vale session tracks.
Tony celebrates International Women's Day with a selection of music from the greatest Queens of Soul - including classics from Aretha Franklin, Diana Ross, Jaki Graham and more!
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1985.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1998.

programmes.show_name: Neil Sedaka: The Music of My...
programmes.show_description: ...Life - Johnnie Walker Meets Neil Sedaka. In tribute to Neil Sedaka who has sadly passed away, another chance to hear him in conversation with Johnnie Walker at the BBC Radio Theatre.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Frank Ifield, The Chiffons and The Rockin' Berries. This week, the top three comes from 1963.
Adeel Akhtar joins Dermot to chat about his new West End role. Plus, we mark 90 years of the Spitfire. And of course The Mystery Voice.
Romesh brings the laughs, soundtracked by great music. As well as chatting to brilliant guests, he's sharing your hilarious stories, so you can start your Saturday with a smile!
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma and enjoy a menu full of your favourite music, great conversation and a delicious dive into the BBC archives for dessert.
Rylan is joined by The Appleton sisters for a very special KaRYoke, plus Mummy Linda's favourite author Kimberley Chambers pops by to talk about her new book.
Shaun Keaveny sits in with fabulous teatime tunes and your tales of the extraordinary and the everyday.

programmes.show_description: Sananda Maitreya shares his favourite tracks on My 80s and there's a Soul Sistas Mastermix.

programmes.show_description: Join Fearne for a nostalgia drenched celebration of the best 90s music and pop culture.
